title: Night
content:
All from the light of the sweet moon
     Tired men lie now abed;
 Actionless, full of visions, soon
     Vanishing, soon sped.

 The starry night aflock with beams
     Of crystal light scarce stirs:
 Only its birds - the cocks, the streams,
     Call 'neath heaven's wanderers.

 All silent; all hearts still;
     Love, cunning, fire fallen low:
 When faint morn straying on the hill
     Sighs, and his soft airs flow.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
